metadata.task: Determine which surface gives the block a higher acceleration when pushed.
metadata.description: Work from the Frictional Force Variation scene state rather than from the title alone. Query accessible object parameters and, when the task involves motion, collisions, forces, or oscillation, step the simulator and compare measured states before submitting the numeric answer.

expected_behavior: The LLM observes that the block accelerates more on the surface with lower friction.
reasoning: Less friction means less opposing force, so the block accelerates more quickly. Surface1 (object_2) has friction 0.2 vs surface2 (object_3) with friction 1.0.
